steps.title: Identify the vertex from the graph
steps.content: The parabola reaches its peak at $x = 2$, which is the vertex. For a downward-opening parabola, the graph increases to the left of the vertex and decreases to the right of the vertex.

steps.title: Check which interval is entirely in the increasing region
steps.content:
• A (0 to 2): Entirely to the left of the vertex—the graph is increasing throughout.
  • B (0 to 4): Crosses the vertex at $x = 2$, so it includes decreasing values.
  • C & D: Both start at or after the vertex, entirely in the decreasing region.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
